checks.a_analysis: The output JSON consists entirely of text and information present in the source document. No fabricated paragraphs, sentences, or introductory/concluding text were found. All section headings and body text correspond directly to the content of the PDF.

All contact information was verified. The name (Dave Head), organization (NC Division of Parks and Recreation), address (1615 Mail Service Center, Raleigh, NC 27699-1615), phone number ((919) 707-9310), and email (dave.head@ncparks.gov) are exactly the same in both the source and the output. The URL provided in section 9 (http://doa.state.nc.us/PandC/actions.htm) also matches the source.

A thorough comparison of the 9-page source PDF and the JSON sections shows that all pages and sections are represented. The conversion successfully captured the document from the program title on page 1 through to the final headings 13 and 14 on page 9.

The document contains one table, the 'Compliance Requirements Matrix' on page 4. The source table contains a header row of numbers (1-14, skipping 11), a header row of requirement names, and a data row of Y/N indicators. The JSON table contains all 13 columns and 3 rows corresponding to the source data.

Sections were checked for completeness. For example, Section I (Program Objectives) correctly merged text across pages 1 and 2. The scoring criteria lists in Sections II and III are complete and accurate to the source. All statutory references and sub-points in Section 1 (Activities Allowed or Unallowed) are fully preserved.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: The output accurately reflects the wording of the source. Formatting choices like the bold/underline on 'not' and 'can' in the auditor instructions on page 1 were correctly captured as semantic flags in the JSON. Minor omissions of document footer codes (e.g., 'C-4 DNCR-9 1') are acceptable reformatting typical for this conversion pipeline.

checks.a_analysis: The output correctly handles document headers and footers, which appear on every page of the source PDF. The document title and department information are rendered once. The contact information for Dave Head, which appears twice on page 1 of the source, is correctly represented twice in the output paragraphs. No unintended duplication of body paragraphs or list items was found.

The heading hierarchy is logical and consistent. The main title is H2, followed by department information as H2. Major sections (I, II, III) are handled via the JSON structure's subsection titles, and the numbered requirements within Section III are correctly identified as H3. No skips in hierarchy were found.

The compliance matrix table on page 4 is correctly converted. The output maintains the 13-column structure (1-14 with 11 missing, as in the source). It accurately captures the dual-row header (numbers and labels) and the data row containing 'Y' and 'N' values.

The reading order follows the logical flow of the source document across all 9 pages. Content that spans page breaks (e.g., from page 1 to page 2 in Section I) is correctly joined in the output. The placement of the table within Section III also matches the source's intended order.

The document contains several complex nested lists (e.g., the scoring criteria on pages 2-3 and the detailed allowed activities in Section III). The output faithfully preserves the nesting levels and numbering/bulleting schemes of the source.
All contact emails (dave.head@ncparks.gov) and web URLs (http://doa.state.nc.us/PandC/actions.htm) from the source are present in the output text. No missing or fabricated links were identified.

checks.a_analysis: The HTML output maintains the visual organization of the source PDF. The multi-column contact information from the first page of the PDF is rendered in a single-column layout in the HTML, which is a standard responsive design practice and does not affect the meaning or flow. Sections and headings follow the same sequential order as the source document.

The 'Compliance Requirements Matrix' table found on page 4 of the source PDF is correctly rendered in the HTML. Although the styling differs (the PDF uses vertical text for headers while the HTML uses horizontal text), the rows and columns are properly aligned, and the data (the 'Y' and 'N' values) is clearly associated with the correct headers. A sighted user can easily read and understand the table data.

The source document primarily consists of text and a table. There are no meaningful content-bearing images (such as charts, diagrams, or photos) that are missing from the HTML output. The 'LGC Reviewed' stamp at the top of the PDF is an administrative header that does not constitute meaningful body content for conversion.

There is no evidence of duplicated body content. While titles and headings appear in both the navigation sidebar (Table of Contents) and the main body, this is an intentional accessibility feature. The actual body text and table content are only rendered once.
